frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decisions arise from gradual integration of sensory pulses over time

frameworks.name: Poisson pulse stimulus design
frameworks.one_line: discrete randomly-timed pulses enable model-based dissection of integration

frameworks.name: virtual reality navigation paradigm
frameworks.one_line: head-fixed mice navigate a virtual T-maze to report decisions

core_question: Can head-fixed mice in virtual reality perform an accumulation-of-evidence task amenable to computational dissection?
key_assumptions: decision reflects integration of discrete sensory pulses
Poisson-timed pulses yield a well-controlled yet diverse stimulus set
mice can serve as a model organism for accumulation tasks given genetic and optical tool availability
manipulations: number and side of visual pulses along virtual T-maze stem
key_findings: mice across genotypes learn the task at rat-comparable levels
mice are sensitive to single-pulse side differences
decisions integrate multiple pulses across the cue region with small early-cue overweighting
performance depends on evidence magnitude but not duration
interpretations: mice genuinely accumulate sensory evidence rather than relying on single cues
the task is suitable for computational and circuit-level study of perceptual decision making in mice
